channel.title: Studio Notes
channel.description: Occasional notes from the studio. Printmaking, collage, found materials, and the slow work of looking.

item.title: Hanging the window wall
item.description: The title work will hang on the window wall at the gallery, in the best afternoon light. I keep moving it in my head before it ever touches a nail.

item.title: What the river leaves
item.description: A torn ticket, a strip of rusted tin, a page softened by weather. I gather along the Delaware most mornings and let the material stay a little dirty. It refuses to be too clean, and that keeps me honest.

item.title: One breath of ink
item.description: Monotype gives a single impression and no edition. The plate keeps a ghost, never the same twice. Teaching it this term reminded me why I trust it: it asks you to commit to a mark.

item.title: Confluence, in three movements
item.description: The lobby triptych reads the meeting of two rivers as three panels: the upstream register, the turning, and the open tide. Industry appears as trace, not subject.

item.title: Reading slowly, on purpose
item.description: Three to four books a month, physical copies only. This month, essays kept by the studio chair and a memoir of objects and memory close to my own work.
